highlights: The board will hear and take action on a Level III employee grievance, the highest internal appeal level in CISD's grievance process.
The board will consider approving an election order to formally initiate an upcoming trustee election.
The board will consider approving an interlocal agreement with UT Austin for the 2026-2027 OnRamps dual enrollment program.
The board will consider authorizing an application for an ESC Region 10 Special Education Funding Transition Grant.

Six board policies are up for first reading, covering purchasing (CH), contracted services (CJ), employee leave (DEC), personnel positions (DP), special education ARD/IEP process (EHBAB), and student discipline (FO, FOA).
Policy GKD (Non-School Use of School Facilities) and the Board Operating Procedures Manual are up for second reading and final adoption.
The board will discuss and may act on updates to the district's cell phone policy implementation.
The board will discuss and may approve the 2026-2027 employee compensation plan, which sets salaries and pay for all district staff.
The board will consider approving a change in the construction delivery method for the Washington High School Phase I project.
The board approved the full consent agenda 7–0, including authorization to apply for the TEA's 2026–2027 RLA Blended Learning Pilot Grant and approval of routine professional personnel employment.

Five new elementary and campus principals were named: Tanya Smith (Birnham Woods Elementary), Amy Barber (Gordon-Reed Elementary), Angela Akin-Fonville (Giesinger Elementary), Lara Vasquez (San Jacinto Elementary), and Dr. Barbara Solberg (Oak Ridge 9th Grade Campus).

The board received a discussion item on student safety policy direction; no vote was taken, but trustees indicated a slate of safety-related policies will be brought for a vote at the June 16th meeting.

General counsel noted the June 16th policy slate will include provisions required by Senate Bill 571, which mandates affidavits and sexual predator registry checks for employees and vendors and prohibits hiring individuals with records of inappropriate conduct.

The superintendent announced the district will consolidate all communication platforms into a single system (ParentSquare) to improve safety, security, and traceability of staff-to-family communications.
The board recognized UIL 6A state champions Camden Gibson (College Park High School, 1600-meter run) and Aiona Chowry (Woodlands High School, girls tennis singles).
The board held two closed sessions covering principal candidates, board governance training, and legal matters; no action was taken in either session.
